records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disentangled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disentangling (?).]

defs: To free from entanglement; to release from a condition of being intricately and confusedly involved or interlaced; to reduce to orderly arrangement; to straighten out; as, to disentangle a skein of yarn.
To extricate from complication and perplexity; disengage from embarrassing connection or intermixture; to disembroil; to set free; to separate.
quotes: To extricate and disentangle themselves out of this labyrinth.
A mind free and disentangled from all corporeal mixtures.
